questions.text: The milk and water in two vessels A and B are in the ratio 4:3 and 2:3 respectively. In what ratio, the liquids in both the vessels be mixed to obtain a new mixture in vessel C containing half milk and half water?
A jar contains a blend of a fruit juice and water in the ratio 5:x. When 1 litre of water is added to 4 litres of the blend the ratio of fruit juice to water becomes 1:1. What is the value of x?

A mixture contains liquid A and liquid B in the ratio of 5:4 respectively. If 2 litres of liquid B is added to it, the ratio of liquid A and liquid B becomes 7:6. What is the quantity (in litres) of liquid A in the mixture?

In a bucket, paint and oil are in the ratio 7:5. 24 litres of mixture is drawn off and 24 litres of oll is added. If the ratio of paint and oil becomes 1:1. then how many litres of paint was contained in the bucket initially?
Rahul adds 2 litres of alcohol in 6 litres of water and Dinesh adds 1 litre of alcohol in 9 litres of water. What is the ratlo of the percentage of alcohol in the two mixtures?
In a mixture of 165 litres, the rallo of liquids X and Y Is 6:5. If 5 litres of liquid Y is added in the mixture, then what is the ratio of X and Y in the new mixture?
The ratios of copper to zinc in alloys A and B are 3:4 and 5:9 respectively. A and B are taken in the ratio 2:3 and melted to form a new alloy C. What is the ratio of copper to zinc in C?

Alloy A contains copper and zinc in the ratio of 4:3 and alloy B contains copper and zinc in the ratio of 5:2. A and B are taken in the ratio of 5:6 and melted to form a new alloy. The percentage of zinc in the new alloy is closest to:

A vessel contains a 32 litre solution of acid and water in which the ratio of acid and water is 5:3. If 12 litres of the solution is taken out and $7\frac{1}{2}$ litres of water is added to it, then what is the ratio of acid and water in the resulting solution?

A milkman uses three containers for selling milk, their capacities being 40 L. 30 L and 20 L respectively. He fills respectively 87.5%, 80% and 90% of the containers with a mix of milk and water in the ratios, 3:2, 5:1 and 7:2 respectively. What is the ratio of the total quantity of milk to that of water carried by him?

In a 56 litres mixture of milk and water, the ratio of milk to water Is 5:2. In order to make the ratlo. of milk to water 7:2, some quantity of milk is to be added to the mixture. The quantity of the milk present in the new mixture wil be:

A and B are solutions of acid and water. The ratios of water and acid in A and B are 4:5 and 1:2, respectively. If x litres of A is mixed with y litres of B, then the ratio of water and acid in the mixture becomes 8:13. What is x:y?
The price of a variety of a commodity is Rs. 7/kg and that of another is Rs. 12/kg. Find the ratio in which two varieties should be mixed so that the price of the mixture is Rs. 10/kg.
Alloy A contains metals xand y only in the ratio 5:2, while alloy B contains them in the ratio 3:4. Alloy C is prepared by mixing alloys A and B in the ratio 4:5. The percentage of x in alloy C is:

Mixture A contains chocolate and milk in the ratio 4:3 and mixture B contains chocolate and milk in the ratio 5:2. A and B are taken in the ratio 5:6 and mixed to form a new mixture. The percentage of chocolate in the new mixture is closest to:

The ratio of milk to water in a 100 litre mixture is 2:3. 10 litres of this mixture is withdrawn and replaced with milk. This process is repeated 2 more times. What is the percentage of milk in the final mixture?

A beaker contains a mixture of two liquids X and Y in the ratio 5:3. When 6 litres of the mixture is drawn off and then replaced with Y, the ratio of X and Y becomes 5:7. How many litres of liquid X was contained in the beaker initially?
In what ratio must a grocer mix two varieties of pulses costing Rs. 15 and Rs. 20 per kg. respectively, so as to get a mixture worth Rs. 18 per kg?
In what proportion must wheat at Rs. 20.40 per kg be mixed with wheat at Rs. 25.50 per kg. so that the mixture is worth Rs. 23.80 per kg?
In what ratio must a shopkeeper mix two types of rice worth Rs 60 per kg and Rs 80 per kg. so that the average cost of the mixture is Rs 75 per kg?
Find the ratio in which a grocer should mix two varleties of pulses costing Rs. 75 and Rs. 90 per kg, respectively, so as to get a mixture costing Rs. 80 per kg.

A beaker is filled with a liquid. 3 parts of which are water and 7 parts some medicine. What part of the mixture should be replaced with water so that the resultant mixture has water and medicine in a ratio 1:1?

Champa buys type 1 sugar at Rs. 45 per kg. He mixes it with type 2 sugar having a price of Rs. 48 per kg. The final mixture becomes worth Rs. 46 per kg. What is the ratio of the quantities of type 1 sugar to type 2 sugar in the final mixture?
Copper and bronze are in the ratio 3:4 in 350 gm of an alloy. The quantity (in gm) of copper to be added to it to make the ratio 4:3 is:
A shopkeeper mixes three types of rice costing Rs. 50 per kg. Rs. 60 per kg and Rs. 75 per kg in the ratio of 3: 1: 2. The average cost of the mixture per kg is:
Rice worth Rs. 96 per kg and Rs. 104 per kg are mixed with a third variety in the ratio 1:1:2. If the mixture is worth Rs. 113 per kg, the price of the third varlety of rice per kg will be:
The cost of two varleties of tea is Rs. 300 and Rs. 375  respectively. If both the varletes of tea are mixed together in the ratio 3 : 2, then what should be the price of mixed variety of tea per kg?
questions.examTag: (SSC SI In Delhi Pollce and Central Armed Police Forces Exam, 09.11.2022 Shift-I)

questions.text: In a mixture of 60 litres, the ratio of milk and water is 2:3. What amount of water must be added to make the ratio of milk and water 1:2?

40 litres of milk is kept in a container. 4 litres of milk was removed from this container and replaced with water. This procedure was performed two more times. How much milk does the container now hold?

A large container has a 50 litre mixture of juice and water in the ratio 3:2. To this, a 60 litre juice and water mixture is added, that has a juice to water ratio of 2:1. After this, 11 litres of the solution is replaced wit pure juice. What is the ratio of water to juice in the final mixture?
A solution of 45% alcohol is mixed with a solution of 60% alcohol in the ratio of 2:3. In what ratio should the resultant solution be mixed with a 72% alcohol solution to get a 66% alcohol solution?

135 kg of an alloy A is mixed with 72 kg of alloy B to get a new alloy. If alloy A has zinc and copper in the ratio 4:5 and alloy B has zinc and copper in the ratio 7:5, then what is the weight of zinc in the new alloy?
In what ratio must water be mixed with milk worth Rs. 50 per litre so as to produce a mixture worth Rs. 35 per litre? (water is available free of cost)

The alcohol and water in a mixture are in the ratio of 4:5 respectively. 20 litres of water is added to it. If the ratio of alcohol and water in the new mixture is 1:3 respectively, then what is the total quantity of the alcohol in the new mixture?

Container I and container II have milk and water in the ratio of 4:5 and 8:3 respectively. In what ratlo the mixfures in container I and container II should be mixed to get a new mixture in which milk and water are in the ratio of 9: 11?
In what ratio rice of price Rs. 20 per kg. be mixed with rice of price Rs. 34 per kg. so that the mixture is worth Rs. 28 per kg?

A cask having a capacity of 600 litres is initially filled with wine. From this cask y litres of wine is first drawn and replaced with water. From this mixture, 120 litres are drawn and replaced with water. From this further diluted mixture 120 litres are drawn again and replaced with water. The ratio of wine to water in the cask is now 12:13. What is the value of y?
In what ratio must a grocer mix two varieties of rice costing Rs. 128 and Rs. 143 per kg, respectively, to get a mixture of rice worth Rs. 137.75 per kg?
A mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 9: 7. If it contains 4 litres more milk than water, then find the quantity of milk (in litres) in the mixture.
In what ratio must a shopkeeper mix two types of rice worth Rs. 55 per kg and Rs. 70 per kg, so that the average cost of the mixture is Rs. 65 per kg?
questions.examTag: SSC Delhi Police SI. CAPFs SI & CISF ASI (CPO) Exam, 03.10.2023 (Shift-II)

questions.text: 80 litres of a mixture of spirit and water in the ratio 7:9 is present in a container A. 20 litres of the mixture is transferred to another container B. Then the container A is filled with 20 litres of water. Then 32 litres of the mixture is again transferred to container B. The ratio of water to spirit of the mixture in the container B is equal to:

One type of liquid contains 20% water and the second type of liquid contains 35% of water. A glass is filled with 10 parts of first liquid and 4 parts of second liquid. The water in the new mixture in the glass is
questions.examTag: (SSC CHSL DEO & LDC Exam.  10.11.2013, IInd Sitting)

questions.text: 20 litres of a mixture contains 20% alcohol and the rest water. If 4 litres of water be mixed in it, the percentage of alcohol in the new mixture will be
7 litres mixture of milk and water contains 30% water. 3.5 litres of milk is added to this mixture. What is the percentage of water in the new mixture?
40 litres of 60% concentration of acid solution is added to 35 litres of 80% concentration of acid solution. What is the concentration of acid in the new solution?

Two bottles of the same capacity are 35% and $33\frac{1}{3}$% full of orange julce, respectively. They are filled up completely with apple juice and then the contents of both bottles are emptied into another vessel. The percentage of apple juice in the mixture is:
A container contains 20 L mixture in which there is 10% sulphuric acid. Find the quantity of sulphuric acid to be added in it to make the solution to contain 25% sulphuric acid.

A vessel contained a solution of acid and water, in which water was 64%. Four litres of the solution was taken out of the vessel and the same quantity of water was added. If the resulting solution contains 30% acid, the quantity (in litres) of the water in the solution, at the beginning in the vessel, was:
80% and 90% pure acid solutions are mixed to obtain 20 litres of 87% pure acid solution. Find the quantity (in litres) of 80% pure acid solution taken to form the mixture.
A mixture of acid and water contains 20 percent acid. When 10 litres of water is added to the mixture, then the percentage of acid becomes 15 percent. What is the original quantity of mixture?
700 grams of cane sugar solution has 60% of sugar in it. How much sugar should be added to make it 75% in the solution?
If 49 litres of a milk solutton has 63% milk in it, then how much milk should be added to make the concentration of milk 70% in the solution?
How much water (in ml) should be added to 300 ml of a 75% milk and water mixture so that it becomes a 45% milk and water mixture?
An alloy contains 40% of silver, 30% of copper and 30% of nickel. How much silver (in kg) should be added to 25 kg of the alloy so that the new alloy contains 50% of silver?
If 80 litres of milk solution has 60% milk in it, then how much milk should be added to make milk 80% in the solution?

A milk vendor has two vessels of milk and water. The first contains 80% milk and the rest is water. The second contains 50% milk. How much mixture (in litre) should he mix from each of the vessels so as to get 9 litres of mixture such that the ratio of water to milk is 1:2? (Write your answer in the order of vessel 1 and vessel 2)
Mixture 1 with a volume of 8 litres has 80% milk and Mixture 2 with a volume of 6 litres has 60% milk. Both the mixtures are mixed. Find the percentage of milk in the fnal mixture.
Rama mixes 20% of kerosene to his petrol and then he sells the whole mixture at the price of petrol. If the cost price of the kerosense is 40% of the CP of petrol. What is the net profit%?

questions.text: In what ratio sugar at Rs. 30 per kg should be mixed with sugar at Rs. 45 per kg so that on selling the mixture at Rs. 42 per kg there is a profit of 20%?
In what ratio, sugar costing ₹ 60 per kg be mixed with sugar costing ₹ 42 per kg such that by selling the mixture at ₹ 56 per kg there is a gain of 12%?

Shovik purchases two types of sugar: the first type at Rs. 45 per kg and the second type at Rs. 80 per kg. In what ratio should Shovik blend the first type of sugar with the second type so that, by selling each kg of the blended sugar at Rs. 72 per kg he makes a profit of 20%?
Suman buys 40 kg of wheat at Rs. 12.50/kg and 30 kg of wheat at Rs. 14/kg. At what rate per kg should he sell the mixture to gain 5% on the whole?
In what ratio, wheat at Rs. 23 per kg should be mixed with wheat at Rs. 14 per kg so that on selling the mixture at Rs. 20 per kg there is a profit of 25%?

There are two varieties of rice. Variety 1 costs Rs. 100 per kg and variety 2 costs Rs. 120 per kg. The vendor mixed both the varieties and sold for Rs. 121 per kg and gained 10%. Find the ratio of variety 1 to variety 2 rice in the mixture.
A milkman mixes water with milk and sells the mixture at the cost price of pure milk. The volume of water in litres to be mixed with each litre of milk to get a 25% profit is
questions.opt4: cannot be calculated without knowing the cost price of milk

questions.text: A drum contains 80 litres of ethanol. 20 litres of this liquid is removed and replaced with water. 20 litres of this mixture is again removed and replaced with water. How much water (in litres) is present in this drum now ?
5 kg of Rs. 18 per kg wheat is mixed with 2 kg of another type of wheat to get a mixture costing Rs. 20 per kg. Find the price (per kg) of the costlier wheat.

A shopkeeper mixes two qualities of rice, which cost him Rs. 40 per kg and Rs. 47.50 per kg respectively, in such a way that he has 15 kg of the blended rice costing him a total of Rs. 645. What is the weight (in kg.) of the rice that cost him Rs. 47.50 per kg.?
In what ratio must a grocer mix two varieties of sweets costing Rs. 30 and Rs. 20 per kg respectively so as to get a mixture worth Rs. 25 per kg?
15 kg of Rs. 25 per kg wheat is mixed with 10 kg of another type of wheat to get a mixture costing Rs. 30 per kg. Find the price (per kg) of the costlier wheat.
In what ratio a trader should mix two varieties of tea one at ₹ 62 per kg and another at ₹ 72 per kg in order to obtain the mixture worth ₹ 65 per kg?
In what proportion may three kinds of tea prices at ₹ 80, ₹ 70 and ₹ 50 per kg be mixed to produce a mixture worth ₹ 60 per kg?
A mixture contains alcohol and water in the ratio 4:3. If 5 litres of water is added to the mixture the ratio becomes 4:5. The quantity of alcohol in the given mixture is:
12 litres of a mixture has wine and water in the ratio 2:3. How much water must be added to get wine to water ratio of 3:7 in the resultant mixture?
55 litres of a mixture has milk and water in the ratio 7:4. How much water must be added to get milk and water in the ratio 7:6 in the resulting mixture.

Two vessels A and B contain milk and water in the ratios 7:5 and 17:7 respectively. In what ratio mixture from two vessels should be mixed to get a new mixture containing milk and water in the ratio 5:3?
Two vessels A and B contain mixtures of milk and water in the ratios 4:1 and 9:11 respectively. They are mixed in the ratio of 3:2. Find the ratio of milk and water in the resulting mixture.
In what proportion may three kinds of rice bought at ₹ 6, ₹ 10 and ₹ 14 be mixed to produce a mixture which would carn 40% on selling it at ₹ 11.20 per kg?
A person has two solutions of sugar with 30% and 50% concentrations respectively. In what ratio should he mix two solutions to get 45% concentration in the resulting mixture?
49 litres of milk has 80% milk concentration. How much water be added to make its concentration 70% ?

3 litres of a mixture containing wine and water in the ratio 3:7 and 4 litres of another mixture containing wine and water in the ratio 3:5 are mixed together. What is the ratio of wine and water in the resulting mixture?

When the market price per kg of rice and wheat be in the ratio 3:2, the monthly expenses of a family towards rice and wheat are in the ratio 5:6. If the market price of rice and wheat becomes in the rattio 4:3. what will be ihe ratio of expenses towards them? (Assume that amount of rice and wheat consumed remains unaltered.)

Three vessels of equal volumes contain water and syrup in the ratio 4:1, 5:2 and 7:3 respectively. When they are thoroughly mixed together in a large vessel, find the resulting ratlo of water and syrup in the mixture. (Assume that in the mixture total volume remains unaltered).

One morning after purchasing 6 litres of milk from a milk man, a householder found that the weight of this quantity of milk was 6.144 kg. If one litre of the pure milk weighs 1.032 kg and that of one litre of the pure water 1 kg. then how much water was added to milk?

While preparing a mixture of tea, 2% tea is lost. In what ratio should a trader mix two kinds of tea containing Rs. 60 per kg. and Rs. 45 per kg. respectively so that he might gain 25% on selling the mixed tea at Rs. 62.50 per kg?

From a pot filled with milk, 20 litres of milk was taken out and filled with same amount of water. Next time 20 litres of mixture was taken out from the pot and again it was filled by same amount of water. If the ratio of milk and water in the pot now be 36:13 find how much milk contained in the pot at first?
A mixture of 125 gallons of wine and water contains 20% water. How much water must be added to the mixture in order to increase the precentage of water to 25% of the new mixture ?

A dishonest grocer professes to sell pure butter at cost price, but he mixes it with adulterated fat and thereby gains 25%. Find the percentage of adulterated fat in the mixture assuming that adulterated fat is freely available.

A vessel is full of refined oil. $\frac{1}{4}$ of the refined oil is taken out and the vessel is filled with mustard oil. If the process is repeated 4 times and 10 litres of refined oil is finally left in the vessel. What Is the capacity of the vessel ?

There are two kinds of alloys of tin and copper. The first alloy contains tin and copper such that 93.33% of it is tin. In the second alloy there is 86.66% tin. What weight of the first alloy should be mixed with some weight of the second alloy so as to make a 50 kg mass containing 90% of tin?
A shopkeeper sells milk which contains 5% water. What quantity of pure milk should be added to 2 litres of milk (containing 5% water) so that proportion of water becomes 4% ?
To m litres of a m% solution of acid, x litres of water is mixed to yleld (m - 10)% solution of acid. If m> 25, then x equals

When one litre of water Is added to a mixture of acld and water. the new mixture contains 20% acld. When one litre of acid is added to the new mixture, then the resulting mixture contains $33\frac{1}{3}$% acid. The percentage of acid in the original mixture was

Radheshyam bought a cow and a buffalo for his dairy. But he thought of selling them off due to some unforeseen circumstances. If he sells the cow at 10% loss and the buffalo at 20% profit, he will have no profit and no loss. But if he sells the cow at a profit of 5% and the buffalo at a loss of 15%, he would lose Rs. 800 in the bargain. How much did Radheyshyam pay for the buffalo?

A vessel contains a mixture of milk and water in the respective ratio of 3:1. 32 litres of mixture was taken out and replaced with the same quantity of milk so that the resultant ratio between the quantities of milk and water in the mixture was 4:1 respectively. If 10 litres of mixture is again taken out from the vessel. what is the resultant quantity of water in the mixture? (in litres)
